title: draba lonchocarpa
intro: Draba lonchocarpa, also known as the alpine whitlow grass or mountain draba, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Draba lonchocarpa include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making it a potential natural remedy for conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Draba lonchocarpa can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's essential oils in topical creams and ointments.
